statement.p: While standing on a rooftop that is 11 m high off the ground, you throw a tennis ball off the roof in an arc. Because of the speed that you throw the ball, its height from the ground, in meters, t seconds after the throw is {-4.9t^{2}+20t+13}\text{.} How long will it be until the ball hits the ground?

solution.p: When the ball hits the ground, the height will be 0\text{.} So we must solve the equation {-4.9t^{2}+20t+13}=0\text{.} Using the quadratic formula:
Only the solution t\approx{4.65194} makes sense in this situation. So it will takes {4.65194\ {\rm s}} for the ball to hit the ground.
